paragraphs.text: Hello, Imaginate that you have a favourite book, but every time you want to open it, someone mandated by the publishing house must do so for you. One day, this person no longer comes, as the publishing house decided to reassign its resources elsewhere. You can no longer open your favourite book. This book that you have bought, but the content of which you can no longer appreciate. This seems ridiculous and yet this is what happens with video games. By decisions of their publishers, video games can no longer be appreciated. We understand the economic needs of not being able to maintain systems forever, but it would not be complex to provide those who so wish with a way to continue these games. Video games are about culture. Help us preserve it. You can find more information by looking for the Stop Killing Games campaign. You can also find a list of games by looking for “Stop Killing Games wiki” (now at least 389 may not work anymore and 358 do not work anymore). Thank you in advance for your consideration.
